docs.title: The Great Camp Crawl
docs.text:
Welcome to the working docs site for The Great Camp Crawl.

This knowledge base is built around venue-level records for overnight programs across the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

docs.title: What this project tracks
docs.text:
• overnight summer camps
  • specialty residential camps
  • sports, arts, academic, and music camps
  • family camps
  • church and faith-based camps and retreats
  • college-run pre-college residential programs

docs.title: Core inclusion rules
docs.text:
A candidate should only become a final record when it has evidence for:

  1. overnight or residential participation
  2. a specific physical venue or session location
  3. activity within the last 24 months

docs.title: Why venue-level files
docs.text:
A single operator may run: - multiple camps - multiple campuses - multiple session venues

This repository keeps one Markdown file per physical venue so pricing, age ranges, dates, and local details can be tracked accurately.

docs.title: Priority lenses
docs.text: The current research bias favors: - college-run pre-college residential programs - one-week-plus programs - maximum breadth before deep optimization

docs.title: Repository references
docs.text:

docs.title: Discovery Pipeline
docs.text: The repository now includes a deterministic raw-discovery pipeline for seed search, evidence capture, normalization, indexing, and staging.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r scripts/requirements-discovery.txt

docs.title: Main entrypoint
docs.text:
Use scripts/run_discovery_pipeline.py for most runs.

Example:

python scripts/run_discovery_pipeline.py \
  "site:.edu pre-college residential program" \
  --run-id us-college-precollege-seed \
  --country US \
  --region MA \
  --program-family college-pre-college

For nationwide college pre-college discovery, use the region fanout wrapper instead of trying to do the whole country in a single prompt:

python scripts/run_college_precollege_nationwide.py \
  --country US \
  --run-prefix us-college-precollege-wave1 \
  --generate-prompt-pack \
  --ingest-after

That wrapper runs one deterministic pipeline pass per region using the college pre-college query angles from scripts/lib/college_precollege_prompt_pack.py. When --generate-prompt-pack is set, it also writes ready-to-paste outside-agent prompts to prompts/discovery/02-college-precollege-scanner-pack/.

Key options:

  • --query-file reads one query per line.
  • --country and --region set discovery defaults for sparse raw records.
  • --program-family controls deterministic query expansion templates.
  • --allow-host-file and --deny-host-file filter search results by host.
  • --search-providers chooses the provider chain for the main runner. Default: instant_answer,lite_html. Use searxng for higher recall when the local SearXNG instance is running on http://localhost:8080.
  • google_cdp is available when local Chrome is already running on localhost:9222; use it when both DDG and SearXNG recall is insufficient.
  • --no-expand disables query expansion.
  • --no-skip-existing-captures forces re-fetching even when a stable capture file already exists.

docs.title: What a run writes
docs.text:
data/raw/discovery-runs/<run-id>/queries.jsonl
data/raw/discovery-runs/<run-id>/search_results.jsonl
data/raw/discovery-runs/<run-id>/capture_manifest.jsonl
data/raw/discovery-runs/<run-id>/run.json
data/raw/evidence-pages/html/*.html
data/raw/evidence-pages/text/*.md
reports/discovery/<run-id>_raw.jsonl
reports/discovery/<run-id>_normalized.jsonl
reports/discovery/<run-id>_followup_queue.jsonl
reports/discovery/<run-id>_split_queue.jsonl
reports/discovery/<run-id>_evidence_index.jsonl
reports/discovery/<run-id>_summary.json
data/staging/discovery-runs/<run-id>/discovered_candidates.jsonl
data/staging/discovery-runs/<run-id>/followup_queue.jsonl
data/staging/discovery-runs/<run-id>/split_queue.jsonl
data/staging/discovery-runs/<run-id>/split_stubs.jsonl
data/normalized/evidence_index.jsonl

docs.title: Script roles
docs.text:
• scripts/search_searxng.py: preferred high-recall seed search via a local SearXNG Docker instance. Same interface as search_duckduckgo.py. Start the instance with docker start searxng.
  • scripts/search_duckduckgo.py: DDG seed search with expansion, retries, timestamps, host filters, query logs, and Lite HTML fallback.
  • scripts/search_batch.py: file-driven wrapper around the same search layer.
  • scripts/html_to_markdown.py: capture one page, preserve HTML, emit Markdown evidence, and append to a manifest.
  • scripts/discover_and_capture.py: lightweight combined path for small search-and-capture jobs.
  • scripts/normalize_existing_discovery_report.py: normalize an existing discovery report into the repo candidate schema.
  • scripts/generate_followup_queue.py: emit deterministic follow-up work items from normalized candidates.
  • scripts/split_multi_venue_candidates.py: emit multi-venue split tasks and split stubs.
  • scripts/capture_to_evidence_index.py: build an evidence index for a run or the full Markdown evidence corpus.

docs.title: Reruns
docs.text:
• Stable capture filenames are based on normalized URLs.
  • By default, existing capture files are skipped on rerun.
  • Run summaries are written to both data/raw/discovery-runs/<run-id>/run.json and reports/discovery/<run-id>_summary.json.
  • The global evidence index can be rebuilt at any time with:
python scripts/capture_to_evidence_index.py data/raw/evidence-pages/text --output data/normalized/evidence_index.jsonl

docs.title: Existing discovery reports
docs.text:
The seeded reports/discovery/us_candidates_2026-04-13.jsonl report is now supported by code-driven companion files:

  • reports/discovery/us_candidates_2026-04-13_normalized.jsonl
  • reports/discovery/us_candidates_2026-04-13_followup_queue.jsonl
  • reports/discovery/us_candidates_2026-04-13_split_queue.jsonl
  • reports/discovery/us_candidates_2026-04-13_split_stubs.jsonl

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• DuckDuckGo Instant Answer is not exhaustive. The Lite HTML fallback improves recall, but it is still a seed-discovery layer rather than a guaranteed complete index. SearXNG returns significantly more results per query and should be the default when available.
  • Content extraction is heuristic. It is built for auditability and repeatability, not perfect page rendering.
  • Multi-venue split generation operationalizes the problem, but venue-specific filling still needs later validation work.
  • Normalization is intentionally conservative; ambiguity is preserved instead of guessed away.

docs.title: Methodology
docs.text: The Great Camp Crawl is a breadth-first research system.

docs.title: 1. Discovery
docs.text: Use country, region, specialty, directory, and language-specific discovery agents to surface candidate programs.

docs.title: 2. Validation
docs.text: Confirm that each candidate is: - overnight or residential - tied to a real physical venue - active within the last 24 months

docs.title: 3. Enrichment
docs.text: Fill in pricing, duration, ages, grades, language, contact details, operator identity, and taxonomy.

docs.title: 4. Rendering
docs.text: Write one final Markdown dossier per venue.

docs.title: Discovery posture
docs.text: This project prefers over-inclusion at the candidate stage and stricter filtering at the final dossier stage.

docs.title: Language posture
docs.text: Discovery should preserve English, Spanish, and French source material when relevant. Filtering to English-only can happen later.

docs.title: Auditability
docs.text: Raw evidence pages and discovery notes are intentionally retained so records can be re-checked year to year.

docs.title: Current focus
docs.text:
• college-run pre-college residential programs
  • programs lasting one week or longer
  • records suitable for later filtering around one child’s needs

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: The Great Camp Crawl tracks overnight and residential youth programs across the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

docs.title: Included
docs.text:
• traditional overnight camps
  • specialty camps
  • sports camps
  • arts camps
  • music and band camps
  • academic and STEM camps
  • family camps
  • faith-based camps and retreats
  • college-run pre-college residential programs

docs.title: Current inclusion thresholds
docs.text: A final record should only be created when there is evidence for: - overnight or residential participation - a specific physical venue or session location - activity in the last 24 months

docs.title: Biases by design
docs.text: This repository intentionally gives higher attention to: - college-run pre-college residential programs - programs lasting one week or longer

docs.title: Current exclusion examples
docs.text:
• day camps without overnight evidence
  • online-only programs
  • venue rentals without a youth program
  • stale or purely historical programs without recent activity evidence

docs.title: 1. Discovery
docs.text:
Run the deterministic code-first discovery pipeline by geography, program family, and language.

Primary outputs: - reports/discovery/<run-id>_raw.jsonl - reports/discovery/<run-id>_normalized.jsonl - reports/discovery/<run-id>_followup_queue.jsonl - data/raw/discovery-runs/<run-id>/... - raw source captures in data/raw/evidence-pages/

docs.title: 2. Validation
docs.text:
Validate overnight status, venue identity, and recent activity.

Primary outputs: - data/staging/validated-candidates.jsonl - data/staging/rejected-candidates.jsonl

docs.title: 3. Enrichment
docs.text:
Fill missing pricing, age ranges, grades, duration, contact details, and taxonomy.

Primary outputs: - data/staging/enrichment-queue.jsonl - data/normalized/*.jsonl

docs.title: 4. Rendering
docs.text: Write one venue dossier per final record into camps/... and refresh browsable docs indexes.

docs.title: 5. QA
docs.text: Run frontmatter validation, duplicate checks, stale checks, and index rebuilds.

docs.title: Year-over-year reuse
docs.text: Keep raw evidence whenever practical so records can be re-validated in future years rather than rediscovered from scratch.

docs.title: United States Coverage
docs.text: The United States is expected to be the largest country slice in the project.

docs.title: Priority areas
docs.text:
• college-run pre-college residential programs
  • well-known overnight camp states and regions
  • specialty camps with one-week-plus sessions

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: Use state-by-state discovery plus university-specific discovery to improve recall.

docs.title: Grounding Rules
docs.text: These rules apply to every discovery, validation, enrichment, and rendering agent in this repository.

docs.title: Mandatory rules
docs.text:
1. Do not mark a program as qualifying unless there is evidence of overnight or residential participation.
  2. Do not infer overnight status from photos, cabins, dorms, rustic branding, or the word camp alone.
  3. Each final record must map to one physical venue or session location.
  4. A record must have evidence of activity in the last 24 months.
  5. Prefer official sites as the primary source of truth.
  6. Directory sites may be used for discovery, but should not be the only source when official evidence is available.
  7. Preserve non-English source material when found.
  8. Do not exclude a camp at discovery time just because the source is Spanish or French.
  9. Record uncertainty explicitly. Do not invent missing facts.
  10. Pricing can be blank during early-stage enrichment, but missing pricing must be flagged.
  11. College-run pre-college residential programs deserve extra recall effort.
  12. Programs lasting one week or longer should be tagged and prioritized.

docs.title: Rejection rules
docs.text: Reject a candidate from final-record status when: - it is only a day camp with no overnight evidence - it is only a venue rental, retreat center listing, or campground without a child or teen program - it has no evidence of recent activity within the last 24 months - the location cannot be tied to a specific physical venue or session site

docs.title: Evidence expectations
docs.text: For every final record, capture source URLs for: - overnight evidence - recent-activity evidence - duration evidence when available - pricing evidence when available

docs.title: Auditability
docs.text: Store raw evidence pages and discovery notes whenever practical so the record can be rechecked in later years.

docs.title: Output Schema
docs.text: Final venue dossiers should use YAML front matter followed by structured Markdown sections.

docs.text:
• record_id: unique identifier for this venue record
  • camp_id: brand-level camp identifier
  • venue_id: physical venue identifier

docs.title: Candidate lifecycle
docs.text:
• discovered — found by a discovery agent but not yet validated
  • validated — passed overnight, venue, and recent-activity checks
  • rejected — does not meet requirements for a final record
  • enrichment_pending — validated but missing important fields
  • rendered — venue dossier written
  • stale_review — requires human or automated recency re-check

docs.title: Activity status values
docs.text:
• active_recent — evidence of activity in the last 24 months
  • possibly_active — weak current evidence, needs follow-up
  • stale — no acceptable recent-activity evidence
  • closed_or_inactive — evidence suggests the program is no longer operating

docs.title: Confidence values
docs.text:
• high
  • medium
  • low
